frameworks.name: Knowledge Compounding Architecture
frameworks.description: Knowledge compounds when new learning connects to existing mental models rather than sitting in isolation. Connection density — not volume — determines compound rate.
steps: Build mental models before facts: frameworks for understanding a domain make every subsequent fact 10x more memorable and useful
Link new knowledge explicitly to existing knowledge — ask 'how does this connect to what I already know?'
Teach to learn: explaining forces connection-making and exposes gaps. Teaching is the fastest compounding accelerator.
Revisit and re-examine: returning to fundamental concepts with new knowledge creates new connections
Identify your core mental model library — the 20-30 models you apply repeatedly across domains
Track: which mental models do you reach for most? Those are your highest-value models to deepen
antipatterns: Consuming content without synthesis — information without connection is not knowledge
Breadth without depth — shallow familiarity with many domains produces no real mental models
No retrieval practice — knowledge not retrieved fades; it does not compound

frameworks.name: Learning Velocity Protocol
frameworks.description: Learning speed is trainable. The bottleneck is rarely intelligence — it is technique. Systematic learning protocol produces 3-5x velocity improvement over naive reading and forgetting.
steps: Pre-learn: before engaging with material, generate questions you want answered — primes retrieval pathways
Active recall over re-reading: close the book and recall what you just learned. Retrieval is the learning event.
Spaced repetition: review at increasing intervals (1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 21 days). Defeats the forgetting curve.
Interleaving: mix topics within a learning session rather than blocking. More difficult, but produces stronger retention.
Minimum effective dose: what is the smallest amount of this material that captures 80% of the value? Learn that first.
Output-first learning: define what you will create or do with the knowledge before consuming — dramatically improves retention
antipatterns: Re-reading as review — feels like learning, produces no retention improvement
Highlighting without recall — passive engagement does not create retrievable memory
Marathon single-session learning — spaced repetition beats massed practice for retention

frameworks.name: Insight Capture System
frameworks.description: Insights not captured are lost. Most people have their best thinking while doing something else. A systematic capture system turns ephemeral insight into compound intelligence.
steps: One capture tool: single location for all raw ideas and insights. Friction kills capture.
Capture immediately: insight half-life is minutes. Do not trust memory.
Review weekly: process raw captures into connected notes or action items
Connect on capture: when adding a new note, link it to at least one existing note
Periodically synthesize: every month, review recent captures and ask 'what pattern is emerging across these?'
Output regularly: writing, teaching, or creating from your insight library accelerates compounding by forcing synthesis
antipatterns: Multiple capture tools — context-switching kills capture habit
Capture without review — inbox that is never processed is not a system
Collection without connection — notes that do not link to other notes do not compound

prompts.name: Mental Model Audit
prompts.template: Audit my mental model library. Domains I have studied seriously: [list]. Models I reach for repeatedly: [list or describe]. Identify my 3 highest-value mental models, the domains where I have shallow familiarity without real models, and the single model that would most amplify my current stack.

prompts.name: Learning Velocity Protocol Design
prompts.template: Design a learning protocol for: [subject]. Time available: [hours/week]. Current knowledge level: [describe]. Output I want to produce: [describe]. Apply pre-learning, active recall, and spaced repetition structure. Give me a week-by-week learning architecture.

prompts.name: Insight Capture System Design
prompts.template: Design my insight capture system. Current capture behavior: [describe — what I use, how often I review]. Primary contexts where I have insights: [describe — reading, walking, conversations]. Give me a minimal, high-friction-free capture and review system.

examples.title: Accelerated Domain Entry
examples.context: Operator entering a new domain (no prior knowledge) needing functional competence in 60 days
examples.approach: Week 1: Read 3 foundational books with active recall after each chapter. Week 2-3: Teach back concepts to colleague. Week 4-8: Apply in low-stakes context, capture insights, connect to existing mental models.
examples.outcome: Functional competence in 60 days. Deep competence in 12 months via continued spaced repetition.

vocabulary.definition: A reusable framework for understanding a category of situation. The base unit of compound intelligence.
Deliberately retrieving information from memory without looking at the source. The primary mechanism of durable learning.
Reviewing material at increasing intervals timed to just before forgetting. Maximizes retention per unit of review time.
Mixing different topics within a learning session. More difficult than blocking but produces stronger retention and transfer.
Ebbinghaus's finding that memory decays exponentially without review. Spaced repetition is the systematic response.
The number of links between a piece of knowledge and the rest of your knowledge base. High connection density = high recall and application.
The smallest amount of input that produces the target output. Applied to learning: capture 80% of value from 20% of material.
The self-reinforcing loop where each new insight makes existing knowledge more valuable and new learning faster. The cognitive equivalent of compound interest.

sources.title: Make It Stick
sources.relevance: Active recall, interleaving, spaced repetition — cognitive science of durable learning

master_laws: Law 19 (Compound — intelligence is the original compound asset)
Law 8 (Focus — deep learning requires protected attention)
sovereign_needs_served: Legacy (compound intelligence outlasts any single achievement)

4_gate_validation.true: PASS — grounded in cognitive science literature
4_gate_validation.high_leverage: PASS — learning velocity is a meta-skill multiplying all other skill acquisition
